params.name: Two-headed Serpent
params.description:
On Nov 3, 1519 AD, the Two-headed Serpent was crafted by an Aztec craftsman.
The Two-headed Serpent is an Aztec artifact made from wood and adorned with turquoise mosaic, spondylus, and conch shell. Believed to have been part of a ceremonial costume, this carving was a symbol in Aztec religion, associated with deities like Quetzalcoatl. The piece is a testament to the Aztec's artistic and cultural achievements, as the civilization was destroyed by invaders, leaving behind only their crafts.
